applicability_notes: Replaces the unfused two-op subsequence seen in call sites/tests: gate = ttnn.silu(gate); hidden = ttnn.mul(gate, up). SiLU is fused into the binary mul via input_tensor_a_activations=[ttnn.UnaryOpType.SILU]. Standard SwiGLU MLP fusion (gate*up before down projection). Same fusion mechanism supports other unary ops on input A (e.g. self.activation_type).

memory_config.shard_spec_template: WIDTH_SHARDED matching gate/up projection output sharding (mul preserves layout)

placement_observations.source: decode path: hidden_sharded = ttnn.mul(gate_sharded, up_sharded, input_tensor_a_activations=[ttnn.UnaryOpType.SILU], memory_config=self._decode_gate_up_out_memcfg); also memory_config=ttnn.L1_WIDTH_SHARDED_MEMORY_CONFIG variant
prefill MLP: w2_in = ttnn.mul(w1_out, w3_out, input_tensor_a_activations=[ttnn.UnaryOpType.SILU], dtype=ttnn.bfloat8_b, memory_config=ttnn.DRAM_MEMORY_CONFIG)

placement_observations.size_descriptor: interleaved SwiGLU hidden carried through mem_cfg shared with gate/up linears
placement_observations.source: TG MLP prefill: w2_in = ttnn.mul(w1_out_reduced, w3_out_reduced, input_tensor_a_activations=[SILU], dtype=ttnn.bfloat8_b, memory_config=w1_out.memory_config()); branch comment: 'For shorter sequence lengths (seq_len < 4096 or batch_size > 1) use the original matmul'
